failure_classes.free_output: claim-boundary diagnosis plus the smallest receipt gate that would have blocked the claim
minimum_evidence: system that should have verified it
failure_classes.name: Receiptless completion
failure_classes.operator_signal: Agent says done, fixed, verified, sent, deployed, or ready before an independent receipt exists.
failure_classes.paid_upgrade: Failure Census if the same class appears across multiple tasks or logs

failure_classes.free_output: redacted artifact or private diagnosis of the side-effect boundary
failure_classes.name: Silent side effect
failure_classes.operator_signal: Agent mutates state, sends, deletes, deploys, buys, or posts without making the blast radius auditable.
failure_classes.paid_upgrade: Contract Install if the workflow needs a deterministic approval or receipt gate

failure_classes.free_output: proof-card showing the difference between internal completion and external receipt
minimum_evidence: time window where the outcome should have appeared
failure_classes.name: Fake-success loop
failure_classes.operator_signal: Automation reports green while the buyer-visible outcome is absent, stale, internal-only, or unverified.
failure_classes.paid_upgrade: Governed Operations if the buyer wants recurring monitoring and remediation

failure_classes.free_output: handoff contract with required return receipts and timeout behavior
failure_classes.name: Handoff gap
failure_classes.operator_signal: Agent hands work to another model, worker, queue, or API and loses accountability for the final state.
failure_classes.paid_upgrade: Contract Install if the handoff sits inside a production workflow

market_mechanic.clearing_price: free triage for one useful failure; paid work only after scope is visible
market_mechanic.demand: teams running agents need proof that operational claims map to receipts
market_mechanic.falsifier: If distributed exchange links produce no qualifying failure in 7 days, the market language is too abstract or the target segment is wrong.
market_mechanic.supply: builders and operators submit real production-agent failures
market_mechanic.target: one externally submitted qualifying failure that becomes a public-safe case artifact

does_not_qualify: benchmark miss with no operator consequence
private incident that cannot be redacted or reviewed safely
broad consulting request with no concrete failure
qualifies: real workflow, not a toy prompt
agent made or implied an operational claim
operator needed another system to verify truth
at least one log, trace, receipt, screenshot, URL, PR, queue item, or deploy record exists

thesis: Shadow is creating a market for agent failures that operators can route into proof: failures enter as concrete traces and leave as receipt gates, public-safe artifacts, or paid audit work.
